title: Buccaferrea × variifolia
taxonomy: Buccaferrea × variifolia is a hybrid species within the family Cunoniaceae. It belongs to the genus Buccaferrea, which is a group of flowering plants native to Australia. The family Cunoniaceae comprises about 25 genera and 400 species of woody plants, primarily found in the Southern Hemisphere. Buccaferrea × variifolia's classification reflects its unique characteristics and genetic makeup as a hybrid species. By classifying it within the Cunoniaceae family, scientists can understand its evolutionary relationships with other species within this family.
uses: Buccaferrea × variifolia, also known as the tropical or Queensland mulberry, has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Its bark and leaves are said to have various medicinal uses, including treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of its medicinal properties. The plant is also used in some parts of the world to treat gastrointestinal issues and as an antiseptic. Further studies are necessary to fully understand the potential medicinal uses of Buccaferrea × variifolia.
benefits: Buccaferrea × variifolia is known to offer various health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing swelling and pain. It is also believed to possess antioxidant properties that can protect the body from oxidative stress and cell damage. Additionally, Buccaferrea × variifolia may have antimicrobial properties that can help in fighting infections. More research is needed to fully understand the extent of its health benefits.

parts: Buccaferrea × variifolia is a plant species known for its potential medicinal properties. Buccaferrea × variifolia is used in traditional medicine, and its medicinal parts are primarily the roots, bark, and leaves. preparations: Buccaferrea × variifolia is an important medicinal plant in traditional medicine, particularly in Australia.  Its herbal preparations have been used to treat various health issues, including fever and rheumatism.
